summary: Terminal pair-programming agent focused on Git-based code edits, repo maps, and broad model support.
implementation_notes: Best quick path for local pair programming and lightweight automation. Very easy to trial.
risk_notes: Not designed as a full multi-agent governance platform. Sandboxing and policy controls are mostly external.

summary: Narrow review-harness/plugin style project centered on Claude Code workflows.
implementation_notes: Small surface area can be useful for a deterministic review loop, not for a full orchestrator.
risk_notes: Very low traction, narrow vendor focus, and no strong sandbox or orchestration layer of its own.

summary: Autonomous coding agent delivered as SDK, IDE extension, CLI, and automation surface.
implementation_notes: Strong practical DX and human approval model. Good when the product needs editor/CLI/SDK surfaces together.
risk_notes: Sandboxing is more approval/checkpoint oriented than hard isolation. Enterprise controls must be configured deliberately.

summary: OpenAI terminal coding agent with OS-level sandboxing, approvals, subagents, skills, hooks, and GitHub Action integration.
implementation_notes: Excellent secure local/CI runner if OpenAI dependence is acceptable. Less suitable as a fully provider-neutral framework.
risk_notes: Provider center of gravity is OpenAI. Some extension and multi-agent surfaces may still evolve quickly.

summary: Batteries-included agent harness with tools, filesystem, permissions, code execution, memory, subagents, and human-in-the-loop controls.
implementation_notes: One of the strongest foundations for a custom Python orchestrator, especially when LangGraph/LangSmith integration matters.
risk_notes: Not a turnkey coding product by itself. Production use requires designing tools, permissions, sandbox backends, and evaluation loops.

summary: Programmable TypeScript agent harness with sessions, tools, skills, sandboxing, deployment targets, and observability adapters.
implementation_notes: Strong candidate for building a product-specific orchestrator in TypeScript. It has a clearer framework shape than a CLI wrapper.
risk_notes: Beta API surface and Node.js >=22.19.0 requirement. Durable persistence requires choosing and operating a backing store.

summary: General-purpose local AI agent with desktop, CLI, API, MCP extensions, and broad provider support.
implementation_notes: Good local automation substrate when tasks extend beyond code and MCP extension breadth matters.
risk_notes: Native observability and coding-specific orchestration are lighter than specialized coding frameworks.

summary: Minimal Python SWE agent for solving GitHub issues or command-line tasks, optimized for simplicity and benchmark reproducibility.
implementation_notes: Best scaffold for learning, ablations, and small research experiments. Very easy to reason about.
risk_notes: Minimal platform features by design: weak native governance, persistence, multi-agent, and observability compared with larger frameworks.

summary: Verification-native local coding-agent runtime with eval gates, memory, subagents, and model profiles.
implementation_notes: Interesting claims for verification-first design, but evidence and traction are too thin for a primary adoption bet.
risk_notes: Minimal traction and no release at retrieval time. Treat as a reference idea, not a dependable platform.

summary: Local multi-model AI coding agent CLI with ReAct, Plan-Execute, MCP, memory, and TUI concepts.
implementation_notes: Potentially hackable Python CLI, but not a strong base for governed orchestration yet.
risk_notes: No stars at retrieval time, early release, and weak evidence for sandboxing, CI, observability, or enterprise controls.

summary: Meta-harness for orchestrating multiple coding-agent backends with policies, sandboxing, and collaboration.
implementation_notes: Compelling for a control-plane strategy where multiple agent harnesses must be swapped or combined.
risk_notes: Alpha status and broad scope make it riskier than narrower libraries. Operational complexity can be high.

summary: Open-source asynchronous coding-agent framework built on Deep Agents and LangGraph, with cloud sandbox providers and automatic PR creation.
implementation_notes: Strong fit for internal async coding-agent platforms. It is more platform starter than simple CLI.
risk_notes: Requires operating sandbox providers and integration surfaces. Hosted examples have historically leaned Anthropic, so model portability should be verified for the target stack.

summary: Open-source coding agent for terminal, desktop, and IDE workflows with broad provider support.
implementation_notes: Very strong for local developer experience and model portability. Less policy-first than sandbox-oriented frameworks.
risk_notes: Native sandbox/governance is lighter than Codex, Sandcastle, Open SWE, or OpenHands SDK; isolation often depends on the surrounding environment.

summary: Self-hosted developer control center for running coding agents and automations locally or in the cloud.
implementation_notes: Best evaluated as a control plane/UI layer, not as the core agent algorithm. It can run OpenHands, Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, or ACP-compatible backends.
risk_notes: Young project with low star count. The actual security boundary is determined by backend selection and deployment topology.

summary: Composable Python and REST SDK for building software-engineering agents with local or ephemeral workspaces.
implementation_notes: A strong Python base for building custom software agents where coding-specific tools, workspaces, and benchmark orientation matter.
risk_notes: Less minimal than Aider or mini-SWE-agent. Teams must still define policies, toolsets, and deployment boundaries.

summary: TypeScript library for orchestrating coding agents in isolated sandboxes with branch/worktree strategies.
implementation_notes: Best when the target is a programmable TypeScript runner around existing coding agents. Docker, Podman, Vercel, and no-sandbox modes make the isolation model explicit.
risk_notes: Young beta project. Examples are strongly tied to Claude Code, and security depends on the chosen sandbox provider, mounts, and network policy.

summary: Research-grade and practical harness for solving real GitHub issues with a model of choice.
implementation_notes: Strong when benchmarkability, issue-resolution tasks, and trajectory logs matter more than polished product UX.
risk_notes: Research-first developer experience. Less suitable as a low-friction daily coding assistant or enterprise control plane.
